agent_reply: - Added [tools/generate_version_source.py](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/tools/generate_version_source.py), which regenerates `src/version.c` before every build so `__DATE__` / `__TIME__` are fresh.
- Updated [platformio.ini](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini) to run that generator as a pre-build script.
- If RTC is invalid, or compile time is newer, writes compile time to the physical RTC.
- Otherwise syncs the software clock state from the existing RTC.
Verification: `pio run` passed. I also ran a second build and confirmed PlatformIO recompiled `src/version.c.o` again, so the pre-build regeneration is doing its job.

user_prompt: edit `tools\generate_version_source.py`, the current string is ok if `git` is somehow unavailable. If git is available on the build PC, the first check if the current git commit has a tag name, use it if so. Otherwise, use 8 characters of the commit hash.
agent_reply: - Uses an exact tag on `HEAD` when available: `git describe --exact-match --tags HEAD`
- Falls back to the short 8-char commit hash: `git rev-parse --short=8 HEAD`
- Falls back to the old `"Built " __DATE__ " " __TIME__` string if git is unavailable or fails
Verified with `pio run -e theflybringuptest`: build succeeds.

Done. I fixed the `platformio.ini` path and the build fallout around the HCI SCO config.
- Added the HCI SCO Bluetooth sdkconfig overrides in `platformio.ini`.
- Added a pre-build script to generate the managed component cert `.S` files PlatformIO expected.
- Removed/renamed project headers that were shadowing libsodium internals:
- Fixed M5GFX’s missing ESP LCD include path.
- Fixed `lib/bluedroid` so the mSBC decoder sources and includes actually build/link.
- Removed the stale `--wrap=log_printf` linker flag since ESP Insights is now disabled.
There are still a few third-party warnings, but no build errors.
